commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content: Brightness. Septuagint, "But thy prayer, like the day-star and life, shall arise to thee from the south, or as at noon-day. "Hebrew, "Thy age (Haydock) shall appear clearer than the noon-day, and darkness like the morning. "Prosperity shall succeed, (Calmet) when thou shalt think all lost. (Menochius)

commentaries.author: Gregory The Dialogist
commentaries.content: 33. For ‘the noonday splendour at eventide’ is the renewing of virtue in the season of temptation, that the soul should be reinvigorated by the sudden heat of charity, which but now was full of fear, that the light of grace had sunk to it; which Zophar further unfolds with more exactness.

methodology.step_1: Parse main page with BeautifulSoup for structure
methodology.step_3: Use Firecrawl to get full content from 'Go to Commentary' links
methodology.step_4: Fallback to BeautifulSoup for accordion content if needed
advantages: Records verses even without commentaries for complete tracking
